perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Affiliate" of a Party means (i) the parent company thereof or (ii) any Person directly or indirectly controlled by, controlling, or under common control with that party (for the purposes of this definition, ownership of fifty percent (50%) or more of the stock, equity or property of such Person, or having the right to appoint fifty percent (50%) or more of the members or owner representatives of such Person are examples of forms of control).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Affiliate" of a Party means any entity with a business relationship with said party, regardless of ownership. This includes, but is not limited to, partners, contractors, and any entity that collaborates on projects or initiatives with the party. For avoidance of doubt, ownership is not a requirement for affiliate status.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Affiliate" has been changed to broaden its scope beyond entities with ownership control to include any entity with a business relationship. This contradicts the original intent of defining affiliates based on control (50% ownership), leading to uncertainty regarding which entities qualify as affiliates and which sections of the contract apply to them. Specifically, it affects clauses that refer to affiliates having the same rights and obligations as the contracting party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.7 "BP GROUP" means the following Persons, individually and collectively: BP and its Affiliates and the officers, directors, employees, agents, and representatives of all of those Persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Affiliate" broadens the scope significantly beyond the original control-based definition. This directly contradicts the definition of "BP GROUP" in section 1.7, which explicitly includes 'BP and its Affiliates'. By redefining 'Affiliate' to include entities with mere business relationships, the composition of 'BP GROUP' becomes vastly different and encompasses entities not originally intended. This creates a clear conflict, making it unclear which entities fall under the BP GROUP umbrella and thus potentially liable under clauses referring to that group.

perturbation.original_text: Company shall have forty-eight (48) hours (exclusive of Saturday, Sunday and holidays) from receipt of the notice to elect, by written notice, whether it will participate in accordance with Section 2.3. BP shall not Complete the Initial Well until Company has notified BP in writing whether or not it will participate or until forty-eight (48) hours (exclusive of Saturday, Sunday and holidays) have elapsed since Company's receipt of BP's notice. Failure to respond within the time period allowed shall be deemed to be an election not to participate in the Completion of the Initial Well.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall have forty-eight (48) hours (inclusive of Saturday, Sunday and holidays) from receipt of the notice to elect, by written notice, whether it will participate in accordance with Section 2.3. BP shall Complete the Initial Well at its own discretion and cost, and Company shall be immediately billed for its portion once work is complete. Failure to respond within the time period allowed shall not be deemed to be an election to not participate in the Completion of the Initial Well.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the decision-making process for the completion of the well. Originally, Company had 48 hours (excluding weekends/holidays) to elect participation, and lack of response was considered non-participation. Now, Company is automatically billed regardless of response, and weekends/holidays are included in the 48-hour window. This in-text contradicts the initial process and creates uncertainty about Company's control over costs and participation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 If BP proposes to Complete the Initial Well and Company timely elects to participate in such Completion attempt in accordance with Section 2.2(A), Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well and 8.75% of the cost of any newly acquired surface equipment associated with the Initial Well beyond the wellhead connections (including but not limited to stock tanks, separators, treaters, pumping equipment, piping, and metering devices).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text both explicitly state that Company must timely elect to participate in the completion of the well. If they elect to do so, they will pay a percentage of the costs. The changed text states the company will be billed for its portion, regardless of response and now includes holidays/weekends, removing the "election" component and making the company automatically responsible for the costs regardless of whether or not they "timely elects to participate". This directly contradicts the contradicted text, specifically with the requirement that the Company timely elects to participate in the Completion attempt in accordance with Section 2.2(A).

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP and Company shall enter into an operating agreement attached hereto as Exhibit "F" (the "Operating Agreement") covering the Contract Area. The Operating Agreement shall be executed contemporaneously with the Partial Assignment but shall be effective on October 1, 2006. The Operating Agreement shall apply to all Additional Wells. In the event of any conflict between the Operating Agreement and this Agreement, this Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP and Company shall enter into an operating agreement attached hereto as Exhibit "F" (the "Operating Agreement") covering the Contract Area. The Operating Agreement shall be executed contemporaneously with the Partial Assignment but shall be effective on the Effective Date. The Operating Agreement shall only apply to the Initial Well. In the event of any conflict between the Operating Agreement and this Agreement, the Operating Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the effectiveness and scope of the Operating Agreement. Originally, it was effective retroactively (October 1, 2006) and applied to all Additional Wells; now, it's effective on the Effective Date (January 1, 2007) and only applies to the Initial Well. Also the governance is changed, originally this agreement shall govern, but due to this perturbation, the Operating Agreement shall govern in the event of any conflict between the Operating Agreement and this Agreement, this in-text contradicts previous statement and creates ambiguity about the rules for additional wells.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Notwithstanding Section 6.1, if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, this Agreement shall continue for so long as the Operating Agreement remains in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that in the event of conflict between the Operating Agreement and the main Agreement, the main Agreement governs. The changed text in Section 5.5 now states that in the event of such a conflict, the Operating Agreement governs. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section. Further, Section 5.5 originally stated that the Operating Agreement applies to all Additional Wells. It is now changed to only apply to the Initial Well. Section 6.2 states 'this Agreement shall continue for so long as the Operating Agreement remains in full force and effect.' Since the change limits the Operating Agreement to the Initial Well, it creates uncertainty about the continuation of the main agreement, thus contradicting Section 6.2.
